post.description:
Spike — pull AgentCore sessions on demand via CloudWatch Logs Insights.

DEV-964 (M14-01). Behind ``settings.enable_agentcore_pull_sync``;
returns 404 when the flag is off so the spike PR is safe to merge
without changing the existing push pipeline. See
``docs/architecture/agentcore-ingestion-direction.md`` for the
decision and POC measurements.

Pull historical conversations from every Bedrock host on a project.

DEV-708 (M9-02). Synchronous (the gateway walks AgentCore Memory →
Sessions → Events on the customer's behalf). Returns an aggregate
``{synced, skipped, errors}`` shape — a single misconfigured host
surfaces in ``errors`` rather than aborting the rest of the sync.

Classic Bedrock Agents hosts produce an entry in ``errors`` with a
"Sync not supported for classic Bedrock Agents" message — they have
no server-side session-list API.

Run one batch of a checkpoint-driven Bedrock sync.

The FE drives a loop against this endpoint so a long sync issues many
short HTTP calls (each capped server-side) instead of a single
multi-minute request. Each call advances one host one batch, persists
the new sessions, and returns the resume cursor plus per-batch
counters for the progress indicator. The loop continues while
``has_more`` is true; when the current host completes, the response
names the next host so the next call advances directly to it.

Cancelling between calls leaves all already-persisted sessions and
checkpoints intact; resuming starts from the persisted checkpoint
automatically when the FE omits it on the first call.

Add or remove tags on every session matching ``body.selection``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Supports both Mode A (ids list) and Mode B
(filter + excluded_ids) via the ``selection.mode`` discriminator.
Single ``UPDATE`` per request.

Server-streamed CSV export of every session matching ``body.selection``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Uses ``StreamingResponse`` + a chunked async
generator so a million-row export never materialises in either FE
or BE memory. The cursor is closed on consumer abort.

Materialise Examples from matching Sessions into ``dataset_id``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Batches of 100 inside one logical operation; the
dataset version bumps once at the end (per the M3 PRD bump matrix).

Persist (or append to) a Session captured at gateway invoke time.

DEV-755 (M9-04 follow-up). Called by the gateway after each
successful Bedrock invoke request/response. The backend deduplicates
on ``(project_id, bedrock_session_id)``:

* First call for a ``bedrock_session_id`` creates a fresh Session
  with ``origin_kind='synced'`` and
  ``source_metadata.connector_path='invoke'``.
* Subsequent calls append turns (idx monotonic) to the existing
  row instead of spawning a duplicate.

The endpoint is best-effort from the gateway's perspective —
failures don't block the user-visible InvokeAgent response. We
still return a typed JSON body so the gateway can emit a useful
log breadcrumb.

Process TrustAI-owned Firehose/S3-delivered AgentCore log objects.

The AWS invoker reads each delivered S3 object body and posts the object
metadata + payload here. The API resolves the project/region mapping and
delegates to the DEV-905 replay service so receipt diagnostics and
raw-event idempotency stay centralized.

post.description:
Calibrate an evaluator against a dataset column's human annotations.

DEV-662 / M4-08 — every annotated row on the
``(dataset_id, column_key)`` pair becomes one calibration sample.
The route pre-fetches the dataset, target column, and joined rows
+ non-null annotations from ``datasets.repository``; the service
layer (``EvaluatorService.calibrate_against_dataset_column``) does
the LLM judging, F1 math, persistence, and version bump.

Raises:
    CalibrationDatasetNotFoundError (404) — the dataset id is unknown.
    CalibrationColumnNotFoundError (404) — the column key is unknown
        on this dataset.
    CalibrationColumnTypeNotSupportedError (422) — column is one of
        score / axial / multi_tag / comment (deferred past M4-08).
    CalibrationNoAnnotationsError (422) — no non-null annotations
        exist on this column yet.
    EvaluatorNotFoundError / StructuredMatchNotSupportedError —
        propagated from the service layer.

Send a user message to Trust Agent and stream the reply.

Default path (``PADDINGTON_STREAMING_ENABLED=True``): returns
``text/event-stream`` and emits events per EDD §3.3. When the client
reconnects with a ``Last-Event-ID`` header, the server replays any
events from persisted turns with ``seq > last_event_id`` instead of
starting a new turn.

Fallback path (flag off): returns the M1 non-streaming JSON response
``RunTurnResponse``. Retained so tests that want a deterministic JSON
shape can opt out of SSE parsing.

post.description:
Non-streaming M1 fallback, always available.

**NO TOOL INVOCATION — TEXT ONLY.** This endpoint runs a single
non-streaming LLM call with no tool loop (see
``task_service.run_turn_stateless``). The model cannot emit real
``tool_use`` blocks here; any tool-shaped JSON in the response is
prose, not a real tool call. Do NOT use this endpoint for
user-facing chat UI — route through ``POST /tasks/{id}/turns``
(the streaming endpoint) instead.

Kept alive for:

* Tests that want a deterministic one-shot JSON response.
* The legacy ``apps/web/src/services/task-service.ts`` mutation
  path, which is scheduled for migration to SSE.

Emits ``X-Paddington-Tools-Disabled: true`` on every response so
callers can observe the limitation without reading this docstring.
Invocations also fire an ERROR-level log in the service layer —
grep for ``paddington.run_turn_stateless.invoked`` in logs to find
accidental callers (DEV-576 / M8-08).

Apply a user decision (approved/rejected) to a pending write-tool approval. Wakes the harness coroutine awaiting on the in-memory future so the turn can resume; if no waiter is registered (server restart, SSE disconnected), the row still gets persisted and the next reconnect via Last-Event-ID will surface the resolved state.

Project-routing answer for a CloudWatch log group lookup (DEV-972).

Used by the Lambda forwarder to resolve

of from a baked-in ``TRUSTAI_AGENTCORE_PROJECT_MAP`` env var. The

Detail-page response: the base row plus a small `recent_*` preview.

the same shape (just empty by default) means the frontend can use a

AgentVersionResponse.title: AgentVersionResponse
AgentVersionResponse.description:
Wire shape for an agent version row.

DEV-1026 V1 widens the response with rollup fields the new top-level
Agent versions page renders inline:

- ``session_count`` — total sessions pinned to this version (any
  origin: synced / uploaded / simulator).
- ``eval_run_count`` — total evaluation runs pinned to this version.
- ``last_activity_at`` — most recent ``created_at`` across the union
  of pinned sessions + eval runs, or ``None`` when this version has
  neither yet.

Defaults keep the schema backward-compatible: callers that don't need
the rollup (e.g. the simulator picker) just ignore the extra fields,
and DB paths that don't compute them (legacy fixtures, fast-path
creates) get sensible zero values.

is set internally by the harness and cannot be requested by a

``approved_scope`` (PAD-M3-05 / DEV-462) optionally widens the grant
to cover subsequent writes that fit a declared plan scope — shape
per ADR-0012 §5. Ignored when ``decision != 'approved'``.

AvailableToolResponse.description:
A tool in the registry the admin UI can surface for restriction.

BulkAddToDatasetRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/datasets/{dataset_id}/examples/bulk``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Materialises Examples (DatasetRows + ExampleTurns)
from the matching Sessions in batches inside one transaction. Bumps
the dataset version once at the end (per the M3 PRD bump matrix).

The ``selection`` envelope re-uses the same Sessions narrowing —
bulk-add-to-dataset reads from Sessions on the source side, so the
discriminator and filter shape both come from the Sessions domain.

new_version.title: New Version

Body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/export``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Server-streamed CSV export. The endpoint returns
a ``StreamingResponse`` with chunked transfer encoding so a million

BulkTagRequest.title: BulkTagRequest
BulkTagRequest.description:
Body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/bulk-tag``.

DEV-746 (M8-33). Single endpoint covers both Mode A and Mode B by
discriminating on ``selection.mode``. Implementation is one
``UPDATE`` per request — never a loop of per-row PATCHes.

Idempotent: adding a tag that's already on a session, or removing
one that isn't, is silently accepted (the SQL ARRAY ops dedupe).

updated_count.title: Updated Count
updated_count.description: Number of session rows touched by the UPDATE.

BulkTagResponse.title: BulkTagResponse
BulkTagResponse.description:
Response from the bulk-tag endpoint.

Returns the count of rows the SQL UPDATE actually touched. Note
that idempotent no-ops (e.g. adding a tag that's already present
on every matching row) still count as touched — Postgres can't
cheaply tell us "this row was updated to the same value." The
count is therefore an upper bound on rows that changed; it's
a lower bound on rows that *matched*.

input_tokens.title: Input Tokens
input_tokens.description: Uncached prompt tokens billed at full rate.

cache_creation_tokens.title: Cache Creation Tokens

cache_read_tokens.title: Cache Read Tokens
cache_read_tokens.description: Tokens served from cache on a hit (~0.1× base rate).

cache_hit_rate.title: Cache Hit Rate
cache_hit_rate.description: Fraction 0–1 of cacheable prompt tokens that came from the cache: cache_read / (cache_read + input_tokens). Higher is better; the M7 probe baseline for Paddington turn-2+ is ~0.9.

savings_usd.title: Savings Usd
savings_usd.description: Estimated USD saved vs. a cache-less baseline. Computed as cache_read_tokens × (base_input_price − cache_read_price) for Sonnet at $3/1M in, $0.30/1M read — directional, not billing.

CacheWindow.title: CacheWindow
CacheWindow.description:
Prompt-cache usage over one time window (DEV-559).

Aggregated across every Paddington assistant turn in the window by
summing the cache fields Bedrock returns on the response — the
harness persists them in ``task_turns.token_usage`` already, so
this is a pure DB read with no extra instrumentation.

CalibrateEvaluatorInput.title: CalibrateEvaluatorInput
CalibrateEvaluatorInput.description:
Input for ``POST /evaluators/{id}/calibrate`` (DEV-662 / M4-08).

Calibration runs the evaluator against the human annotations stored
on a specific column of a dataset. Each annotated row becomes one
calibration sample: the human annotation is the ground truth, the
evaluator's verdict on that row's example content is the prediction.

M4-08 ships ``pass_fail`` support only; other column types
(``score`` / ``axial`` / ``multi_tag`` / ``comment``) raise
``CalibrationColumnTypeNotSupportedError`` until follow-ups land.

CreateDatasetColumnInput.title: CreateDatasetColumnInput
CreateDatasetColumnInput.description:
Body for ``POST /datasets/{dataset_id}/columns``.

The route layer hands the raw ``config`` dict to the service, which
validates it against the per-type config model (above). We keep
``config`` typed as ``dict[str, Any]`` here rather than a discriminated
union because pydantic-style discriminator wiring at this layer
couples the ``type`` field to a fragile ``Annotated[Union[..]]``;
delegating to the service keeps the validation rules in one place.

``key`` is the stable machine identifier — must match
``[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_]*`` so it's safe to use in URLs and JSON keys.

CreateDatasetRowAgentStepInput.title: CreateDatasetRowAgentStepInput
CreateDatasetRowAgentStepInput.description:
Agent execution metadata copied onto a row-created ExampleTurn.

This is the single-row counterpart to the sessions bulk materializer:
clients that already have a session detail payload can send
human-readable turns plus their trace/tool metadata in one request
instead of falling back to legacy ``input_data`` pointer blobs.

CreateDatasetRowInput.title: CreateDatasetRowInput
CreateDatasetRowInput.description:
Body for ``POST /datasets/{dataset_id}/rows``.

Per M3-11 (DEV-660), the legacy ``input_data`` / ``expected_output``
JSONB columns were dropped from storage. They're still accepted on
the wire as a backwards-compat affordance: when supplied, the
service translates them into one user turn + one assistant turn
+ ``io_split = 1`` and writes them through the new
``ExampleTurn`` schema. New surfaces can supply ``turns`` +
``io_split`` directly so the row is evaluation-ready immediately.

CreateDatasetRowTurnInput.title: CreateDatasetRowTurnInput
CreateDatasetRowTurnInput.description: A materialized turn copied into a newly-created dataset row.

enable_reasoning.title: Enable Reasoning
enable_reasoning.description: Enable chain-of-thought reasoning for LLM Judge calls. Off by default (fast, extractive). Turn on for nuanced judgments — adds significant latency and token cost.

CreateEvaluatorInput.title: CreateEvaluatorInput
CreateEvaluatorInput.description:
Input for creating a new evaluator.

LLM Judge evaluators must supply a prompt. Structured Match evaluators
have fixed comparison logic and must not supply one — they pin a
``subkind`` (``routedTo`` | ``toolsUsed``) to choose the comparison
field.

DEV-681 / M7-03: ``evaluator_type`` was retired here in favour of
the ``kind`` / ``subkind`` pair (DEV-643 / M4-01) — the underlying
column has been dropped from ``evaluation.evaluators``.

``idx`` is optional. When omitted, the new turn is appended at the
end. When provided, existing turns at ``idx`` and beyond are shifted
right by one to make space — this is the only way to *insert* in the
middle of the sequence.

DatasetRowResponse.description:
Wire shape for a DatasetRow.

Post-M3-11 (DEV-660), the legacy ``input_data`` / ``expected_output``
JSONB columns were dropped from the database. This response shape
keeps the keys for backwards compatibility — they're populated
server-side from the row's turns + ``io_split`` via
:mod:`legacy_io` when the service layer serialises the row.
Frontend callers that already render those keys (the dataset
detail row preview, the evaluation result panel) keep working
without a change. New surfaces should prefer ``turns`` +
``io_split`` directly.

DatasetVersionEventListResponse.title: DatasetVersionEventListResponse
DatasetVersionEventListResponse.description:
Paginated wire shape for ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/events``.

``items`` is in ``created_at DESC`` order — newest event first —
so the timeline UI can render top-down without re-sorting.
``total`` reflects the count *after* the optional ``kind`` filter
is applied so paging math is correct in either mode.

DatasetVersionEventResponse.title: DatasetVersionEventResponse
DatasetVersionEventResponse.description:
Wire shape for one row in ``dataset_version_events``.

Emitted by ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/events`` and consumed by
the dataset version-history viewer (M6-13). Mirrors the columns
on :class:`DatasetVersionEvent` exactly — no service-layer
reshaping. Clients dispatch on ``kind`` to decide how to render
the ``before_json`` / ``after_json`` slices.

The ``row_id`` field is logically a reference to ``DatasetRow.id``
but intentionally NOT a foreign key — the audit log survives row
deletions so historical bumps stay visible. Clients should treat
a missing row gracefully.

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: One-line description of the persona to draft.

DraftPersonaInput.title: DraftPersonaInput
DraftPersonaInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/personas/draft``.

DEV-769 / M11-06: AI-assisted drafting. The user supplies a one-line
goal ("frustrated long-time customer trying to cancel"); the service
layer calls Bifrost and returns a populated persona shape the FE can
render into the create form for review. Does NOT persist — saving
goes through the existing CRUD on the user's confirmation.

EndSimulationResponse.title: EndSimulationResponse
EndSimulationResponse.description:
Returned from ``POST /v1/simulations/{id}/end``.

``session_id`` is the ID the FE redirects to so the operator can
review the just-emitted session.

EvaluationResultDetailResponse.title: EvaluationResultDetailResponse
EvaluationResultDetailResponse.description:
Per-result detail rendered from the frozen ``example_snapshot``.

Per RFC 0003 §7.1 ("Detail endpoint"), this response **must not**
join to live ``dataset_rows`` / ``example_turns`` /
``example_agent_steps`` / ``dataset_row_annotations``. Everything
the detail page renders comes from the snapshot blob captured at
run dequeue time, which means a result row stays renderable even
after its source dataset row is edited or deleted.

``example_snapshot`` is typed as a free-form dict because its
inner shape lives in RFC 0003 — the FE consumes it through a
typed wrapper generated from the OpenAPI schema, but the FastAPI
surface keeps it opaque so future RFC-compatible field additions
don't trigger an OpenAPI / type regeneration churn.

EvaluationRunAgentVersionAggregate.title: EvaluationRunAgentVersionAggregate
EvaluationRunAgentVersionAggregate.description:
One row of the aggregate-by-agent-version response.

Each row represents all evaluation runs in the project pinned to the
same ``agent_version_id``. ``avg_pass_rate`` is the simple mean of
each run's pass rate (``passed_rows / completed_rows``); runs with
``completed_rows == 0`` are excluded from the mean (and surface as
``None`` if no run in the bucket has progressed). The label is
pulled from the ``agent_versions`` table for human display; legacy
runs with ``agent_version_id IS NULL`` report ``None`` for both id
and label.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

EvaluationStatus.title: EvaluationStatus
EvaluationStatus.description: Lifecycle state of an evaluation run.

EvaluatorKind.title: EvaluatorKind
EvaluatorKind.description:
Top-level evaluator taxonomy (DEV-643).

Collapses the 5-value ``EvaluatorType`` into the 2-value PRD shape:
LLM Judge evaluators score with a prompt; structured match evaluators
compare a specific field on the actual turn against the expected turn.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

EvaluatorListResponse.title: EvaluatorListResponse
EvaluatorListResponse.description:
Paginated list of evaluators (DEV-750).

M8-37 added pagination to ``GET /v1/evaluators`` so the FE
Evaluators table consumes the same generic primitives as Sessions /
Datasets / Evaluations. Wraps the ``items`` array in the shared
``{items, page, page_size, total, total_pages}`` envelope.

EvaluatorPin.description:
One ``{id, version}`` entry from ``evaluation_runs.evaluators_pinned``.

Surfaced on ``EvaluationRunResponse`` (DEV-693 / M6-09) so the run
detail page can render a per-evaluator version chip without
making a second round trip to the evaluator-versions endpoint.
The shape mirrors the JSONB row written by the service at run start
(DEV-661 / M4-04).

EvaluatorResponse.title: EvaluatorResponse
EvaluatorResponse.description:
Response schema for an evaluator.

DEV-681 / M7-03: ``evaluator_type`` was removed from this surface
after the underlying column was dropped — clients should read
``kind`` (and, for structured matches, ``subkind``).

EvaluatorSubkind.title: EvaluatorSubkind
EvaluatorSubkind.description:
Which structured field a ``structured_match`` evaluator compares (DEV-643).

Only meaningful when ``kind = structured_match``. ``ROUTED_TO`` covers
the legacy ``intent_match`` / ``skill_match`` types (compares which
skill the router selected); ``TOOLS_USED`` covers the legacy
``tool_call_match`` / ``parameter_match`` types (compares the tools
invoked and their parameters).

EvaluatorUsageResponse.title: EvaluatorUsageResponse
EvaluatorUsageResponse.description: How many evaluation runs currently reference this evaluator.

EvaluatorVersionResponse.title: EvaluatorVersionResponse
EvaluatorVersionResponse.description: Response schema for an evaluator version.

Body of the eviction endpoint. Mirrors a subset of

snapshot was removed.

ExampleAgentStepResponse.title: ExampleAgentStepResponse
ExampleAgentStepResponse.description:
A single agent execution step (route/tool/llm) attached to an ExampleTurn.

Mirrors ``SessionAgentStepResponse``. The dataset side carries an
independent copy of the captured agent steps (``example_agent_steps``
table) so a row's history is preserved even after the source session
is deleted. Shape is identical to the session-side response so the
front-end's ``AgentStepTree`` renderer consumes both interchangeably.

``ord`` is the zero-based intra-turn ordinal — the API guarantees
steps are returned sorted by ``ord ASC`` per turn, so the FE can
iterate without resorting client-side.

agent_steps.title: Agent Steps
agent_steps.description: Ordered agent execution steps captured for this turn, sorted by ``ord`` ASC. Empty list (never null) when the turn has no recorded steps. Steps are read-only from the dataset side: they reflect what the source agent actually did at sync time.

FeatureFlags.title: FeatureFlags
FeatureFlags.description:
Server-side bootstrap flags for FE feature gates.

Anything that the FE needs to know about server gating up front
(so it doesn't render UI behind a route that 404s) goes here.

InternalProviderEventInput.description: Provider event shape consumed from the gateway event sink.

InternalSimulationEventInput.description: Internal event persistence payload sent by the gateway.

LastEvaluationRun.description: Summary of the most recent evaluation run for the project overview.

MentionSearchItem.title: MentionSearchItem
MentionSearchItem.description:
One row in the ``@``-picker search response (PAD-M4-02 / DEV-678).

``type`` is a typed ``MentionKind`` discriminator the FE uses to pick
the right icon + secondary-text formatter. Adding a new kind means
extending the alias above and the dispatch table in
``mentions_service.py`` — not adding a magic string here.

id.title: Id
id.description: UUID or slug of the referenced resource

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Human label shown in the picker

MentionedObject.title: MentionedObject
MentionedObject.description:
One ``@``-picker selection carried into a turn (PAD-M4-02 / FR-53).

Shape mirrors the picker: ``type`` is the resource kind, ``id`` is
the UUID the model must use when calling ``query_data``, and
``display_name`` is the human label the user saw in the dropdown.
The triplet becomes a line in Layer 4 of the system prompt so the
model resolves ``@foo`` against a concrete ID instead of hallucinating.

DEV-678 / M7-12: ``type`` is now a typed ``MentionKind`` literal so
the wire shape matches the FE union and unknown values 422 at the
edge instead of leaking into the system prompt.

PageContext.title: PageContext
PageContext.description:
Automatic floating-chat page context (PAD-M4-02 / FR-52).

Emitted by ``usePageContext()`` on each product page and attached to
every turn submitted from floating mode. All fields are optional so
pages can disclose as much or as little as makes sense — a list page
without a selected object still advertises its visible rows; a detail
page sets ``selected_object`` and leaves ``visible_objects`` empty.

PassRateTrendPoint.title: PassRateTrendPoint
PassRateTrendPoint.description: A single data point in the pass rate trend timeline.

prompt_version.title: Prompt Version
prompt_version.description: Versioned prompt id; bump in code on any structural change.

PersonaDraftResponse.title: PersonaDraftResponse
PersonaDraftResponse.description:
Returned from ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/personas/draft``.

Mirrors the persistable persona fields (``name`` / ``description`` /
``system_prompt`` / ``goals`` / ``constraints``) but in their list
forms for goals / constraints — the AI-assist contract per
DEV-769 returns these as ``list[str]`` so the FE can render
chip-style bullets the user can edit individually before saving.
The user remaps these into the JSONB shape the persistable
``CreatePersonaInput`` expects.

status.description: Health status: green, yellow, or red

recommended_action.title: Recommended Action
recommended_action.description: Suggested action to improve health (e.g. 'add scenarios', 'retrain evaluator', 'review agent')

PolicyHealthItem.title: PolicyHealthItem
PolicyHealthItem.description: Health status for a single evaluator category.

PolicyPackDetailResponse.title: PolicyPackDetailResponse
PolicyPackDetailResponse.description: Full pack detail with categories and policies.

PolicyPackSummaryResponse.title: PolicyPackSummaryResponse
PolicyPackSummaryResponse.description: Lightweight pack listing without nested categories.

ProjectCounts.title: ProjectCounts
ProjectCounts.description: Top-level entity counts for the project overview metrics.

table_count.title: Table Count
table_count.description: Number of in-scope tables that contributed at least one row

size_bytes.title: Size Bytes
size_bytes.description: Approximate size of the JSON payload in bytes

schema_version.title: Schema Version
schema_version.description: Alembic revision of the database schema at capture time

contents.title: Contents
contents.description: Per-domain row counts captured in this snapshot — datasets, sessions, evaluators, evaluation_runs, trust_agent_tasks. Empty dict for snapshots captured before this field existed.

ProjectSnapshotResponse.title: ProjectSnapshotResponse
ProjectSnapshotResponse.description:
Snapshot metadata exposed to the API.

The ``payload`` column is intentionally NOT exposed here — it can
be hundreds of kilobytes of mixed-shape rows from across every
domain in scope, and there is no UI for rendering it.

ProviderUsage.title: ProviderUsage
ProviderUsage.description: Per-provider spend + request rollup across the three standard windows.

RecentRunItem.title: RecentRunItem
RecentRunItem.description: Summary of a recent test run.

ReconcileAgentVersionsInput.title: ReconcileAgentVersionsInput
ReconcileAgentVersionsInput.description:
Request body for ``POST /agent-versions/reconcile``.

DEV-971 / M14-07. Hides AgentCore agent versions whose
``source_metadata.agent_runtime_arn`` doesn't match the project's
configured runtime. Called from the host save path so the simulator
picker shows only the configured runtime's endpoints.

source.title: Source
source.description: Bedrock source surface that produced the invoke turn.

bedrock_session_id.title: Bedrock Session Id
bedrock_session_id.description: AWS-side sessionId on the InvokeAgent call. Forms the idempotency key together with project_id — re-invokes with the same id append turns instead of creating a duplicate row.

host_id.title: Host Id
host_id.description: Gateway host id (optional, captured for traceability).

agent_runtime_arn.title: Agent Runtime Arn
agent_runtime_arn.description: AgentCore runtime ARN for AgentCore invoke capture.

agent_runtime_id.title: Agent Runtime Id
agent_runtime_id.description: AgentCore runtime id for AgentCore invoke capture.

agent_runtime_name.title: Agent Runtime Name
agent_runtime_name.description: AgentCore runtime name for AgentCore invoke capture.

endpoint_qualifier.title: Endpoint Qualifier
endpoint_qualifier.description: AgentCore runtime endpoint qualifier used for invoke capture.

endpoint_name.title: Endpoint Name
endpoint_name.description: AgentCore runtime endpoint name used for invoke capture.

agent_version_id.title: Agent Version Id
agent_version_id.description: TrustAI AgentVersion id that produced this invoke capture, when known.

end_user_id.title: End User Id
end_user_id.description: External end-user id sourced from the caller (e.g. WorkOS subject). Persisted on the Session row so the FE can render a useful 'User' column. Optional.

user_message.title: User Message
user_message.description: Verbatim user-side prompt for this turn.

assistant_message.title: Assistant Message
assistant_message.description: Verbatim assistant response for this turn.

tool_calls.title: Tool Calls
tool_calls.description: Structured AgentCore tool calls captured alongside the assistant response.

RecordInvokeTurnInput.title: RecordInvokeTurnInput
RecordInvokeTurnInput.description:
Body for the internal ``POST /v1/internal/sessions/record-invoke-turn``.

DEV-755. Sent by the gateway after each successful classic Bedrock
Agents ``InvokeAgent`` call so TrustAI's Sessions store reflects
*future* turns proxied through the Trust Agent flow. Complements
DEV-775's CloudWatch sync (which covers historical) and DEV-765's
simulator emission (synthetic). Idempotent on
``(project_id, bedrock_session_id)`` — re-invoking the same Bedrock
sessionId appends turns to the existing Session row.

The endpoint is internal-only: the API service rejects calls without
a matching ``X-Internal-Token`` header. Capture failures are
swallowed by the gateway so they never block the agent invoke from
returning to the user.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: TrustAI Session id (existing or freshly created).

created.title: Created
created.description: True if a new Session row was created; False if an existing row was appended to.

turn_count.title: Turn Count
turn_count.description: Total turn count on the Session after this call.

RecordInvokeTurnResponse.title: RecordInvokeTurnResponse
RecordInvokeTurnResponse.description:
Response from the internal capture endpoint (DEV-755).

Tells the gateway whether a Session row was created vs an existing
one was appended to. Used for log breadcrumbs only — capture is
fire-and-forget from the gateway's perspective.

ReorderDatasetColumnsInput.title: ReorderDatasetColumnsInput
ReorderDatasetColumnsInput.description:
Body for ``POST /datasets/{dataset_id}/columns/reorder``.

``keys`` is the desired display order for the dataset's columns.
The service rejects requests whose ``keys`` aren't a strict
permutation of the dataset's current column keys — adds and removes
must go through the create/delete endpoints so version-bump events
stay clean.

RowAnnotationsResponse.title: RowAnnotationsResponse
RowAnnotationsResponse.description:
Wire shape for ``GET /datasets/{dataset_id}/rows/{row_id}/annotations``.

``values`` is a ``{column_key: value}`` map. Orphaned keys (keys
whose ``DatasetColumn`` was deleted) are filtered out on read so
callers never see schema-orphan junk; the values still live in
Postgres (per the M3-06 EDD's orphan tolerance) so re-creating the
column resurrects them.

page_context.description: Floating-chat page context (PAD-M4-02). Rendered into system-prompt Layer 4.

mentioned_objects.title: Mentioned Objects
mentioned_objects.description: Objects the user explicitly ``@``-mentioned in this turn.

RunTurnInput.title: RunTurnInput
RunTurnInput.description: Input for running a non-streaming turn against Trust Agent.

RunTurnResponse.title: RunTurnResponse
RunTurnResponse.description: Result of a non-streaming turn: the persisted user + assistant turns and assistant text.

SavedViewCreate.title: SavedViewCreate
SavedViewCreate.description:
Body for ``POST /saved-views``.

Server stamps ``created_by`` from the authenticated user and ignores
any client-supplied ``builtin`` flag (always created as ``False``).

SavedViewResponse.title: SavedViewResponse
SavedViewResponse.description: Single SavedView serialized for HTTP responses.

SavedViewUpdate.title: SavedViewUpdate
SavedViewUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /saved-views/{id}``.

Both fields are optional. ``created_by`` is immutable (audit-only)
and ``builtin`` cannot be flipped from the client.

ScoreHistoryEntry.title: ScoreHistoryEntry
ScoreHistoryEntry.description: A single score history data point.

ScoreHistoryListResponse.title: ScoreHistoryListResponse
ScoreHistoryListResponse.description: Paginated list of score history entries for sparkline charts.

run_count.title: Run Count
run_count.description: Number of completed runs that day

ScoreHistoryPoint.title: ScoreHistoryPoint
ScoreHistoryPoint.description: A single data point in the score history timeline.

ScoringType.title: ScoringType
ScoringType.description: How an evaluator reports its score.

ord.title: Ord
ord.description: Zero-based step ordinal within the turn (sorted ASC).

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable label — route name, tool name, or model name.

input.title: Input
input.description: Kind-specific input payload (params for tools, prompt for llm). Nullable.

output.title: Output
output.description: Kind-specific output payload (response for tools, completion for llm). Nullable.

SessionAgentStepResponse.title: SessionAgentStepResponse
SessionAgentStepResponse.description:
A single agent execution step within a turn (M2-02 / DEV-637, wired by DEV-733).

Mirrors a row in the ``session_agent_steps`` table. Shape matches what
the front-end ``AgentStepTree`` renderer at
``apps/web/src/components/features/sessions/agent-step-tree.tsx``
consumes: a discriminator ``kind`` ∈ {route, tool, llm}, a
human-readable ``name``, and optional ``input`` / ``output`` blobs the
expandable tool-call rows render as syntax-highlighted JSON.

``ord`` is the zero-based intra-turn ordinal — the API guarantees
steps are returned sorted by ``ord ASC`` per turn, so the FE can
iterate without resorting client-side.

routed_to.title: Routed To
routed_to.description: Distinct ``route``-kind step names across all sessions in the project. Sourced from ``session_agent_steps`` rows where ``kind = 'route'``. Sorted alphabetically.

tools_called.title: Tools Called
tools_called.description: Distinct ``tool``-kind step names across all sessions in the project. Sourced from ``session_agent_steps`` rows where ``kind = 'tool'``. Sorted alphabetically.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Distinct tag values across all sessions in the project. Same data as ``GET .../tag-vocabulary``, surfaced here so the filter popover can populate every dimension in one fetch.

SessionFilterVocabularyResponse.title: SessionFilterVocabularyResponse
SessionFilterVocabularyResponse.description:
Distinct vocabulary for every supported Sessions filter dimension.

DEV-752 (M8-39). Powers the Sessions filter popover's level-2
checkbox lists. One round-trip populates every dimension at once
(Routed To, Tools Called, Tags). Each list is alphabetically sorted
server-side so the popover renders a stable order without
re-sorting client-side. Date is omitted — that dimension uses a
range picker, not a vocabulary.

All lists are project-scoped; no values from other projects leak in.
Empty lists (never null) are returned for projects with no matching
data so the popover can render an explicit "no values yet" state
without nullish-coalescing.

total_session_count.title: Total Session Count
total_session_count.description: Total Session rows in this project at response build time (DEV-1013). ``None`` for in-flight imports; populated once the import has completed or failed.

SessionImportResponse.title: SessionImportResponse
SessionImportResponse.description:
Response schema for a session import job.

DEV-1013: ``total_session_count`` is the project's Session row
count when the import finished (computed at response build time —
not stored). The Sync-result dialog reads this so it can render
"Project already has N sessions" on an empty-window run instead of
a bare ``Imported 0 sessions`` line that reads as a failure.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the filter set.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page_size. Always at least 1 — when total is 0 we still return 1 so the FE never renders 'Page 1 of 0'.

SessionListResponse.title: SessionListResponse
SessionListResponse.description:
Paginated list of sessions.

DEV-750 (M8-37). Extends the shared :class:`PageEnvelope` so the FE
generic ``useTablePagination`` hook reads the metadata fields
(``page``, ``page_size``, ``total``, ``total_pages``) by name. The
``items`` field is re-declared with the concrete element type so
the generated OpenAPI schema documents a typed list.

SessionMessageInput.title: SessionMessageInput
SessionMessageInput.description: A single message in a conversation session.

display_id.title: Display Id
display_id.description: Per-project monotonic counter (DEV-741 / M8-28). Internal ordering surface — clients render the human-readable ``display_label`` instead of this raw integer.

end_user_id.title: End User Id
end_user_id.description: External end-user identifier from the customer's source system (DEV-744 / M8-31). For Bedrock-synced sessions this is the AgentCore actor ID. ``null`` for pre-DEV-744 rows and any source that doesn't carry one.

origin_kind.title: Origin Kind
origin_kind.description: Provenance discriminator (DEV-765 / M11-02, surfaced on the response by DEV-778). Tells the FE where this session came from so it can render an origin badge / column without re-querying the filtered list. Backed by the ``ck_sessions_origin_kind`` CHECK constraint at the DB layer.

simulation_id.title: Simulation Id
simulation_id.description: Back-pointer to the simulation that emitted this session (DEV-765 / M11-02, exposed on response by DEV-778). ``None`` for every non-simulator origin and for simulator-emitted sessions whose source simulation has since been deleted (``ON DELETE SET NULL`` at the DB layer).

turns.title: Turns
turns.description: Structured per-turn rows from the session_turns table (M2-01 / DEV-635). Populated only on GET /sessions/{id} when ?include=turns is set; ``null`` everywhere else for wire-compat with the legacy ``messages`` shape. After M7-04 / DEV-687 dropped the legacy ``sessions.messages`` JSON column, ``session_turns`` is the source of truth — ``messages`` is kept on the response as a back-compat projection until the date advertised by the ``Deprecation`` response header.

turn_count.title: Turn Count
turn_count.description: Number of turns in the conversation.

display_label.title: Display Label
display_label.description:
Human-readable session label (DEV-741 / M8-28).

Renders the per-project ``display_id`` counter as
``SES-{display_id:06d}`` — six zero-padded digits. The UI uses
this everywhere a session is shown to a non-technical user
(Sessions table cell, side-panel header, etc.) so they never
see the raw UUID. UUIDs remain available on ``id`` for FK
integrity / deep links.

events_total_estimate.title: Events Total Estimate
events_total_estimate.description: Best-effort projection of total events for this connection. Null when the connector has no actor inventory yet.

actor_count.title: Actor Count
actor_count.description: AgentCore actors visible on this batch.

batch_index.title: Batch Index
batch_index.description: Zero-based batch index, echoed from the request.

SessionSyncBatchDiagnostics.title: SessionSyncBatchDiagnostics
SessionSyncBatchDiagnostics.description:
Non-secret per-batch diagnostics surfaced by the sync-batch endpoint.

Mirrors the gateway diagnostics shape so the FE can render counters
without parsing nested envelopes.

host_id.title: Host Id
host_id.description: Host the FE wants to advance. The first call may omit this; the response then names the next host the loop should resume.

checkpoint.title: Checkpoint
checkpoint.description: Resume cursor returned by the previous batch, or null on the first call.

batch_index.title: Batch Index
batch_index.description: Zero-based index of this batch within the loop. Echoed onto diagnostics for progress UIs.

SessionSyncBatchInput.title: SessionSyncBatchInput
SessionSyncBatchInput.description:
Request body for one batch of a checkpoint-driven Bedrock sync.

The FE drives the loop: each call advances one host one batch and the
response carries the resume cursor plus diagnostics needed for the
progress indicator. Omit every field on the first call.

synced.title: Synced
synced.description: Sessions newly persisted by this batch.

skipped.title: Skipped
skipped.description: Sessions skipped by this batch (already present).

total_session_count.title: Total Session Count
total_session_count.description: Total Session rows in the project after this batch.

host_name.title: Host Name
host_name.description: Display name of the advanced host.

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: Whether the loop should continue. True when this host has more batches OR there are additional unprocessed Bedrock hosts on the project.

next_host_id.title: Next Host Id
next_host_id.description: When ``has_more`` is true and the current host completed, the host the next batch should advance.

checkpoint.title: Checkpoint
checkpoint.description: Resume cursor for the next batch on this host. Null when this host completed.

SessionSyncBatchResponse.title: SessionSyncBatchResponse
SessionSyncBatchResponse.description: Response for one batch of a checkpoint-driven Bedrock sync.

total_session_count.title: Total Session Count
total_session_count.description: Total Session rows in this project after the sync completed (DEV-1013). Computed at response time — not stored. Lets the FE distinguish a genuinely empty project from an empty-window result on a project that already has data.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Human-readable per-host error messages. Empty on full success.

SessionSyncResponse.title: SessionSyncResponse
SessionSyncResponse.description:
Response from ``POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/sync``.

DEV-708 (M9-02). Bedrock session sync runs synchronously per host
and reports an aggregate count of newly persisted sessions plus the
count of duplicates skipped by the dedup check. Per-host errors are
captured in ``errors`` so a partial-success response is still useful
— a misconfigured AgentCore host doesn't block another host's sync.

DEV-1013: ``total_session_count`` reports the project's total
Session row count after the sync completed. The "Sync complete /
New: 0 / Already in TrustAI: 0" empty-window result confused users
when the project already had 26 sessions — the per-run ``skipped``
counter only reflects duplicates seen in *this* run. The total lets
the dialog say "Project already has N sessions" instead of bare
zeros.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Distinct tag values across all sessions in the project, sorted alphabetically. Empty when no sessions in the project carry any tags.

SessionTagVocabularyResponse.title: SessionTagVocabularyResponse
SessionTagVocabularyResponse.description:
Distinct, sorted tag vocabulary for a project (DEV-743 / M8-30).

Returned by ``GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions/tag-vocabulary``.
Source of truth is the union of every session's ``tags`` JSONB array —
there is no separate vocabulary table. The list is sorted
alphabetically (case-sensitive — Postgres default collation) so the
Tag popover renders a stable order without re-sorting client-side.

add.title: Add
add.description: Tags to add. Already-present tags are no-ops.

remove.title: Remove
remove.description: Tags to remove. Absent tags are no-ops.

SessionTagsUpdate.title: SessionTagsUpdate
SessionTagsUpdate.description:
Body for ``PATCH /sessions/{id}/tags``.

Both fields are optional and default to empty lists, so a client can
add only, remove only, or no-op (empty body). The endpoint is
idempotent: adding a tag that already exists or removing one that
isn't present is silently accepted.

idx.title: Idx
idx.description: Zero-based turn index within the session.

reasoning_text.title: Reasoning Text
reasoning_text.description: Extracted reasoning trace (DEV-1018). Populated when the underlying assistant turn carried a ``<thinking>...</thinking>`` block; ``null`` everywhere else. The FE renders this in a collapsed ``<ReasoningTrace>`` disclosure component inside the assistant message bubble — never inline with the prose.

agent_steps.title: Agent Steps
agent_steps.description: Ordered agent execution steps that produced this turn, sorted by ``ord`` ASC. Empty list (never null) when the turn has no recorded steps.

SessionTurnResponse.title: SessionTurnResponse
SessionTurnResponse.description:
A single conversation turn (M2-05 / DEV-653).

Mirrors a row in the ``session_turns`` table (M2-01 / DEV-635). Each
turn carries an ordered ``agent_steps`` array sourced from the
``session_agent_steps`` table (M2-02 / DEV-637) and wired into this
response by DEV-733. The list is empty when the underlying turn has
no steps — never ``null`` — so the FE renderer can render an explicit
"No agent steps" empty state without nullish-coalescing.

start_date.title: Start Date
start_date.description: Inclusive lower bound on session timestamp.

end_date.title: End Date
end_date.description: Inclusive upper bound on session timestamp.

routed_to.title: Routed To
routed_to.description: Match sessions whose agent steps include any of these route names (OR within the dimension). Empty list is a no-op.

tools_called.title: Tools Called
tools_called.description: Match sessions whose agent steps include any of these tool names (OR within the dimension). Empty list is a no-op.

tag.title: Tag
tag.description: Match sessions tagged with any of these tag values (OR within the dimension). Empty list is a no-op.

SessionsFilters.title: SessionsFilters
SessionsFilters.description:
Narrowed filter object that drives the Sessions list query.

DEV-746. The same shape is used by both ``GET /sessions`` (read)
and bulk-ops Mode B request bodies. The repository's
``build_session_filter_clauses`` helper builds the SQL WHERE
clauses from this model; new filters MUST be added in both places
or the read view and the bulk-ops view will drift.

DEV-752 (M8-39): the multi-dimension filter popover threads
``routed_to``, ``tools_called``, and ``tag`` lists through here.
Within a single dimension the values OR (any-match); across
dimensions the dimensions AND. Empty / unset lists are no-ops.

excluded_ids.title: Excluded Ids
excluded_ids.description: Row IDs to subtract from the filter set (the user de-selected these after promoting to all-matching).

SessionsSelectionByFilter.title: SessionsSelectionByFilter
SessionsSelectionByFilter.description: Sessions Mode B: ``SessionsFilters`` + an excluded_ids list.

ids.title: Ids
ids.description: Row IDs to operate on. Empty list is a no-op.

SessionsSelectionByIds.title: SessionsSelectionByIds
SessionsSelectionByIds.description: Sessions Mode A: an explicit list of session IDs.

SetRowAnnotationInput.title: SetRowAnnotationInput
SetRowAnnotationInput.description:
Body for ``PUT /datasets/{dataset_id}/rows/{row_id}/annotations/{column_key}``.

``value`` is intentionally typed as ``Any`` because the legal shape
depends on the column's ``type``:

* ``pass_fail`` -> ``bool``
* ``score``     -> ``int`` / ``float`` (inside the column's range + step)
* ``axial``     -> ``str`` (one of the column's pole keys)
* ``multi_tag`` -> ``list[str]`` (subset of options, unless ``allow_freeform``)
* ``comment``   -> ``str``

The service rejects mismatched shapes with a structured 400.

has_sessions.title: Has Sessions
has_sessions.description: Project has at least one imported session

has_datasets.title: Has Datasets
has_datasets.description: Project has at least one dataset

has_evaluators.title: Has Evaluators
has_evaluators.description: Project has at least one evaluator

has_evaluation_runs.title: Has Evaluation Runs
has_evaluation_runs.description: Project has at least one evaluation run

SetupStatus.title: SetupStatus
SetupStatus.description:
First-run checklist completion flags for a project.

Powers the `Home` checklist that guides new projects through setup.
Each flag is true when at least one record of that type exists for
the project. `has_hosts` is not included here because hosts live in
the separate gateway service; the frontend checks it via a direct
call to the gateway hosts API.

ShareTaskResponse.title: ShareTaskResponse
ShareTaskResponse.description:
Result of ``POST /paddington/tasks/{task_id}/share``.

Idempotent: a second share of the same task returns the same slug.
The FE uses ``shared_slug`` to build the copy-to-clipboard URL
(``${origin}/shared/${shared_slug}``); the full URL is intentionally
not computed server-side so the endpoint stays host-agnostic.

TaskApprovalResponse.title: TaskApprovalResponse
TaskApprovalResponse.description: One ``task_approvals`` row, serialised for the route + SSE payload.

TaskFileContentResponse.title: TaskFileContentResponse
TaskFileContentResponse.description:
Full-content response for the read-on-demand path.

Binary files return ``content=None`` and ``content_base64`` set so
callers can round-trip them as JSON; text files return ``content``
as a plain string so the model can reason over it directly.

preview.title: Preview
preview.description: First N lines of the file for text-ish mime types only.

TaskFileManifestEntry.title: TaskFileManifestEntry
TaskFileManifestEntry.description:
One row of the Layer 5 task-file manifest (EDD §6.1).

Contains metadata + summary + a short text preview. The full file
body is NEVER included here — the model must call a read tool
(e.g. ``query_data(type=file, id=...)``) to fetch the body on demand.

TaskWithTurnsResponse.title: TaskWithTurnsResponse
TaskWithTurnsResponse.description: A Task with its ordered turns embedded for the detail route.

ToolPermissionResponse.title: ToolPermissionResponse
ToolPermissionResponse.description:
One workspace-authored restriction on a tool.

Default behaviour is allow-all — the absence of a row here means
the tool is enabled for every user in the workspace. The admin UI
(follow-up ticket) renders rows in this list as "restricted" and
the ``available_tools`` list returned by the list endpoint as
"enabled for everyone".

score.title: Score
score.description: Trust score 0-100 based on pass rate

failed_runs.title: Failed Runs
failed_runs.description: Runs with at least one failure

TrustScoreSummary.title: TrustScoreSummary
TrustScoreSummary.description: Overall trust score derived from test run pass rates.

UpdateAgentVersionInput.title: UpdateAgentVersionInput
UpdateAgentVersionInput.description:
Request body for ``PATCH /agent-versions/{id}``.

Only the label is mutable. Re-parenting to a different project is
intentionally not supported — agent versions belong to the project
they were created in.

UpdateBudgetInput.title: UpdateBudgetInput
UpdateBudgetInput.description: Input for updating a project's budget cap.

UpdateDatasetColumnInput.title: UpdateDatasetColumnInput
UpdateDatasetColumnInput.description:
Body for ``PATCH /datasets/{dataset_id}/columns/{column_key}``.

``key`` and ``type`` are immutable on update — once a column has
annotation values written against it, renaming the key or changing
the type would silently invalidate every existing value. Callers
that need a different type must delete + create.

subkind.description: Which structured field a structured_match evaluator compares.

enable_reasoning.title: Enable Reasoning
enable_reasoning.description: Enable chain-of-thought reasoning for LLM Judge calls (LLM Judge only).

UpdateEvaluatorInput.title: UpdateEvaluatorInput
UpdateEvaluatorInput.description:
Input for editing an existing evaluator.

Only editable fields are exposed: ``kind`` is fixed after
creation (changing it would invalidate version history and calibration
results). All fields are optional so callers can send a partial update.

Mutating any of ``prompt``, ``criteria``, ``scoring_type``,
``pass_threshold``, or ``subkind`` cuts a new ``EvaluatorVersion`` row
via ``EvaluatorService._bump_evaluator_version()``. Renaming or
rewording (``name`` / ``description``) does not.

UpdateExampleTurnInput.title: UpdateExampleTurnInput
UpdateExampleTurnInput.description:
Patch a turn's content and/or role. ``idx`` is fixed by ordering
operations (insert / delete) and intentionally not patchable here —
callers should delete + insert if they need to reorder.

UpdateIoSplitInput.title: UpdateIoSplitInput
UpdateIoSplitInput.description:
PATCH ioSplit on a DatasetRow.

``io_split`` is a single integer in ``[0, len(turns)]`` — it's the
boundary index that separates "input" turns from the "expected
output" turn(s). The service validates the upper bound against the
row's current turn count.

UpdatePersonaInput.title: UpdatePersonaInput
UpdatePersonaInput.description:
Request body for ``PATCH /v1/personas/{id}``.

Every field is optional; only the supplied keys are updated. Re-parenting
to a different project is intentionally not supported — personas belong
to the project they were created in.

agent_platform.title: Agent Platform
agent_platform.description: Primary agent platform this project will evaluate

emit_paddington_sessions.title: Emit Paddington Sessions
emit_paddington_sessions.description: Whether to capture in-app TrustAI agent (Paddington) conversations as evaluation sessions for this project.

UpdateSkillInput.title: UpdateSkillInput
UpdateSkillInput.description:
Partial update for a skill row. Only provided fields are applied.

``slug`` and ``is_ootb`` are intentionally not editable — rename by
delete+recreate; OOTB status is set once by the seeder and is
disable-only per FR-37.

UpdateTaskInput.title: UpdateTaskInput
UpdateTaskInput.description: Input for partially updating a Task. Only set fields are applied.

capture_trustai_agent_sessions.title: Capture Trustai Agent Sessions
capture_trustai_agent_sessions.description: Whether to capture in-app TrustAI agent conversations. Toggling on creates an auto-managed project; toggling off deletes that project and all its captured data.

UpdateWorkspaceSettingsInput.title: UpdateWorkspaceSettingsInput
UpdateWorkspaceSettingsInput.description: PATCH body. Today the only mutable field is the capture toggle.

UpsertToolPermissionInput.title: UpsertToolPermissionInput
UpsertToolPermissionInput.description:
Body of ``POST /paddington/tool-permissions``.

A single mutation surface for the admin UI's enable/disable toggle and
role gate. Toggling enabled is the common case; ``required_role`` is
accepted today but inert until the role system lands (see DEV-622's
coordinated follow-up ticket). ``tool_name`` is validated against the
live registry server-side — passing an unknown tool returns a 400
rather than silently storing a row that filters nothing.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: One entry per registered provider, in stable slug-alphabetic order

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.default: Cost values are Bifrost's per-call estimates and may differ from the provider's invoice. Use them for relative spend comparison, not billing reconciliation.

success_rate.title: Success Rate
success_rate.description: Fraction 0-100 of calls that returned a 2xx upstream response

average_latency_ms.title: Average Latency Ms
average_latency_ms.description: Mean upstream round-trip latency in milliseconds

total_cost_usd.title: Total Cost Usd
total_cost_usd.description: Bifrost-estimated cost in USD using its pricing datasheet. Directional — NOT authoritative billing; provider invoices remain the source of truth.

UsageWindow.title: UsageWindow
UsageWindow.description: Aggregate totals over a single time window for one provider.

WorkspaceCaptureImpactResponse.title: WorkspaceCaptureImpactResponse
WorkspaceCaptureImpactResponse.description:
Summary used by the confirmation modal when toggling capture OFF.

Counts data that would be removed if the user confirms. The modal
surfaces these to the user so they can make an informed choice
rather than discovering loss after the fact.

WorkspaceSettingsResponse.title: WorkspaceSettingsResponse
WorkspaceSettingsResponse.description:
The full workspace settings shape surfaced to the frontend.

``trustai_agent_sessions_project_id`` is the auto-managed project's
UUID when capture is on, otherwise ``None``. The frontend deep-links
into that project's Sessions list so users have one click to "see
the captured conversations" right next to the toggle.
